context: Tests whether the agent follows the full 5-step progressive enhancement workflow — starting with plain HTML, adding Turbo Frames, adding Turbo Streams, layering Stimulus, and validating at each step including JavaScript-disabled behavior.

checklist.name: Step 1: HTML-first code
checklist.description: The implementation includes or documents a plain HTML version of the feature that works without any JavaScript (standard form_with or link_to with no Turbo-specific attributes initially)

checklist.name: Step 2: Turbo Frame added
checklist.description: turbo_frame_tag is added in a second step to wrap the update region — the code or documentation shows this as a distinct enhancement layer after HTML-first

checklist.name: Step 3: Turbo Stream added
checklist.description: A respond_to block with format.turbo_stream and a .turbo_stream.erb template are added in a third step

checklist.name: Step 4: Stimulus last resort
checklist.description: Stimulus is only introduced (step 4) for behavior that cannot be achieved with Turbo alone — the rationale for why Turbo is insufficient is apparent from the code or comments

checklist.name: JS-disabled verification
checklist.description: The submission explicitly confirms the feature was verified with JavaScript disabled — either through a system test with no_js driver, a documented manual step, or a note in a README/comment

checklist.name: Steps in correct order
checklist.description: The workflow progresses HTML → Turbo Frames → Turbo Streams → Stimulus in that order (not Stimulus before Turbo, not Turbo Streams before Frames)

checklist.name: Per-step validation noted
checklist.description: The implementation documents or notes a validation checkpoint after at least two of the four enhancement steps (not just at the end)

checklist.name: Graceful degradation
checklist.description: The final implementation degrades gracefully: forms submit and data persists correctly when JavaScript is disabled (not just noted but actually implemented)

checklist.name: No full-page frame nav
checklist.description: Turbo Frames are NOT used to drive page-to-page navigation — they scope only the relevant partial-update region

checklist.name: Stimulus only where needed
checklist.description: The Stimulus controller (if present) handles only client-side behavior that Turbo cannot handle (e.g., character counter, drag-drop, clipboard) — not server-round-trip behavior
